creator: Trond.Endrestol@ximalas.info
text:
Here's the result from pkg info -f gcc, if it's of any use:

# pkg info -f gcc
gcc-4.8.5
Name           : gcc
Version        : 4.8.5
Installed on   : Wed Jul 22 19:50:36 CEST 2015
Origin         : lang/gcc
Architecture   : freebsd:10:x86:64
Prefix         : /usr/local
Categories     : lang java
Licenses       : GPLv3RLE and GPLv3
Maintainer     : gerald@FreeBSD.org
WWW            : http://gcc.gnu.org/
Comment        : GNU Compiler Collection 4.8
Options        :
        BOOTSTRAP      : on
        JAVA           : on
Shared Libs required:
        libmpfr.so.4
        libmpc.so.3
        libgmp.so.10
        libasound.so.2
Shared Libs provided:
        libstdc++.so.6
        libssp.so.0
        libquadmath.so.0
        libobjc.so.4
        libmudflapth.so.0
        libmudflap.so.0
        liblto_plugin.so.0
        libjvm.so
        libjavamath.so
        libitm.so.1
        libgomp.so.1
        libgjsmalsa.so
        libgij.so.14
        libgfortran.so.3
        libgcj.so.14
        libgcj-tools.so.14
        libgcc_s.so.1
        libatomic.so.1
Annotations    :
        cpe            : cpe:2.3:a:gnu:gcc:4.8.5:::::freebsd10:x64
Flat size      : 743MiB
Description    :
GCC, the GNU Compiler Collection, supports a number of languages.  This
port installs the C, C++, Fortran and Java front ends as gcc48, g++48,
gfortran48, and gcj48, respectively.

It can be used interchangibly with the lang/gcc48 port which tracks
weekly upstream snapshots whereas this port will be updated less
frequently, mostly in sync with upstream releases, and will move to
lang/gcc49 and later over time.

WWW: http://gcc.gnu.org/

Gerald Pfeifer <gerald@FreeBSD.org>
